tensions.text: Cached edge market sizes for adjacent/overlapping years diverge by nearly 4x (GVR $12.36B in 2024 vs Coherent $46.86B in 2026, both pre-2026 vintage), meaning any absolute-$-based carve-out ratio used to derive seg.edge's ~5% share is highly sensitive to which vendor's edge-market figure is paired with the total DC market denominator.

The total DC market denominator itself varies across sources for nearby years ($386.71B in 2025 per Precedence vs $416.09B in 2024 per TechnologyChecker), compounding uncertainty in any edge-$-over-total-$ carve-out calculation feeding seg.edge.

FMI's cited 34.6% IT&Telecom share is confirmed to apply only to the 'data center power' sub-market (a $15.2B-$31.2B market per FMI, vs GVR's $22.77B-$71.76B estimate for the same nominal sub-market), not the total DC market — reinforcing the node's own caveat that this figure cannot be used as a clean telecom-of-total-DC share, and also showing the power sub-market itself is inconsistently sized across vendors.

Dataintelo's $6.8B telecom power systems market explicitly segments end-users into 'Telecom Towers, Data Centers, and Others,' confirming this figure is broader than data-center-only telecom demand and cannot serve as a clean numerator for a telecom-share-of-DC-market ratio, as the node's promotion path requires.

absences: No source publishes edge data center market size as a percentage/share of the total data center market — every edge report (GVR, Fortune Business Insights, Mordor, Coherent, electroiq, MRFR) reports only absolute USD figures.
No source publishes a telecom-only (excluding towers/off-grid sites) share of the total data center market or of data center power spend.
No vintage-matched pair of edge-market-$ and total-DC-market-$ for the same year/publisher was found, preventing a clean single-source carve-out calculation.
No operator-capex breakdown isolating telecom-operator data-center spend from broader telecom power infrastructure (towers, off-grid sites) was found.
